terms.definition: Python's package installer; downloads and installs packages from PyPI and other indexes.
The Python Package Index, the default public repository pip installs packages from.
A plain-text file listing a project's dependencies and version specifiers, installable with pip install -r.
A constraint on acceptable versions of a package, e.g. ==1.40.0, >=0.2,<0.3, or ~=1.4.0.
Optional dependency groups a package declares, requested with package[extra].
A command that prints all installed packages at their exact versions, useful as a reproducibility snapshot.
